title: CNN This Morning · 2026-07-10 · 麦康奈尔健康状况与政治透明度
summary: CNN独家视频显示参议员麦康奈尔一个月前被担架抬上救护车，其健康状况成谜，共和党同僚称不知其生死。以色列警告美国伊朗计划暗杀特朗普，或解释近期飞机更换及美军打击。纽约危楼抢修工作持续。凯莉·詹娜代言Meta AI眼镜引发隐私争议。

paragraphs.speaker: Cnn Anchor
paragraphs.en: People in California, but not in a good way. This peach truck, it lost some of its fruit on the roadway and caused a major traffic jam. The peaches were eventually pushed to the median, and the lane was thankfully reopened. As if there's not enough that L.A. drivers have to deal with. That does it for CNN HEADLINE EXPRESS. I'm Brad Smith. CNN THIS MORNING with Audie Cornish starts right now.
paragraphs.cn: 加州的人们，但可不是什么好事。这辆桃子卡车在道路上掉落了一些水果，造成了严重的交通堵塞。桃子最终被推到中央隔离带，车道也 thankfully 重新开放了。好像洛杉矶司机要应付的事情还不够多似的。CNN头条快讯到此结束。我是布拉德·史密斯。奥蒂·科尼什主持的《CNN今晨》节目现在开始。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right. We've got new video just into CNN, showing the moment Mitch McConnell was taken to the hospital a month ago. It's bringing up questions about transparency surrounding his health.
paragraphs.cn: 好的。我们刚刚收到一段新的视频，显示米奇·麦康奈尔一个月前被送往医院的那一刻。这引发了关于其健康状况透明度的质疑。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: He's obviously not doing well, but don't know if he's alive or has passed away.
paragraphs.cn: 他显然状况不佳，但不知道他是活着还是已经去世了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And Israel shares intel of an Iran plant -- plan to assassinate President Trump. Does that explain the plane switcheroo and the renewed strikes by the U.S.? Urgent work continues to save that buckling building in New York from partial collapse. Is the timeline too ambitious? And from hot girl summer to hot surveillance summer. Is Meta trying to sweep privacy concerns under the rug by making Kylie Jenner the face of its new A.I. glasses?
paragraphs.cn: 以色列分享了伊朗工厂的情报 -- -- 计划暗杀特朗普总统。这是否解释了飞机调换和美国重新发动空袭的原因？拯救纽约那栋摇摇欲坠的建筑免于部分倒塌的紧急工作仍在继续。时间表是否过于雄心勃勃？从辣妹夏天到监控夏天。Meta是否试图通过让凯莉·詹娜成为其新AI眼镜的代言人来掩盖隐私问题？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: Do not buy the Kylie Jenner Meta glasses. Don't buy them. Point blank, period. They are manufacturing consent.
paragraphs.cn: 不要买凯莉·詹娜的Meta眼镜。不要买。就这么简单，句号。他们是在制造同意。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, this is new and exclusive video just into CNN. It's showing the moment a month ago that Senator Mitch McConnell was loaded into an ambulance. Now, his current health status remains in question. Good morning, everybody. I'm Audie Cornish, and we want to start with the discussion about the former Senate leader's health. CNN has exclusively obtained video of the moment that the Kentucky Republican was taken by ambulance from his home. This happened on June 14.
paragraphs.cn: 这是刚刚传入CNN的独家新视频。它显示了一个月前参议员米奇·麦康奈尔被抬上救护车的时刻。现在，他的当前健康状况仍存疑问。大家早上好。我是奥蒂·科尼什，我们首先讨论前参议院领袖的健康问题。CNN独家获得了这段视频，显示这位肯塔基州共和党人在6月14日被救护车从家中接走。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Neighbors saw the senator being loaded into an ambulance on a stretcher. Two ambulances, a fire truck, and Capitol police officers were also seen blocking off the street. Now, the face of the person isn't visible in the video, but one neighbor told CNN that an eyewitness saw McConnell's face. Now, this is new detail around McConnell's hospitalization, which has been a mystery so far. Many Republican lawmakers on Capitol Hill say they've been kept in the dark. REP. BRETT GUTHRIE (R-KY): We haven't spoken directly.
paragraphs.cn: 邻居们看到这位参议员被用担架抬上救护车。还看到两辆救护车、一辆消防车和国会警察封锁了街道。视频中看不到当事人的脸，但一位邻居告诉CNN，一名目击者看到了麦康奈尔的脸。这是关于麦康奈尔住院的新细节，此前一直是个谜。国会山的许多共和党议员表示他们被蒙在鼓里。众议员布雷特·格思里（肯塔基州共和党人）：我们没有直接交谈过。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I've text with him, and I've talked to people with his office and his staff. And, you know, I don't know much more than what the public knows. SEN. MARSHA BLACKBURN (R-TN): I have not had any conversations with him. I understand that some of my colleagues have. We wish him a speedy recovery. REP. MARLIN STUTZMAN (R-IN): Yes. No, no, I have not heard from him. And I think it's a question that Republicans should be asking.
paragraphs.cn: 我和他发过短信，也和他在办公室的工作人员谈过。你知道，我知道的不比公众多多少。参议员玛莎·布莱克本（田纳西州共和党人）：我没有和他有过任何交谈。我知道我的一些同事有。我们祝愿他早日康复。众议员马林·斯图茨曼（印第安纳州共和党人）：是的。不，不，我没有收到他的消息。我认为这是共和党人应该提出的问题。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: Do you know that he's alive?
paragraphs.cn: 你知道他还活着吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Stutzman
paragraphs.en: I don't. You know, just the things that I've heard and seen from some friends is that he's obviously not doing well, but don't know if he's alive or has passed away.
paragraphs.cn: 我不知道。你知道，我从一些朋友那里听到和看到的是，他显然状况不佳，但不知道他是活着还是已经去世了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, joining me now in the group chat, Elliot Williams, CNN legal analyst and former federal prosecutor; Terry Schilling, president of the American Principles Project; and Lulu Garcia-Navarro, CNN contributor and "New York Times" journalist. One of the reasons why people are talking about this, political people are talking about this in a kind of ghoulish way, frankly, is that the Kentucky Senate vacancy law, meaning what do you have to do if suddenly a Senate seat is open? Well, there's a special election.
paragraphs.cn: 那么，加入我们群聊的有：CNN法律分析师、前联邦检察官埃利奥特·威廉姆斯；美国原则项目主席特里·席林；以及CNN撰稿人、《纽约时报》记者露露·加西亚-纳瓦罗。人们 -- -- 政治人物 -- -- 以一种近乎病态的方式谈论此事的原因之一，坦率地说，是肯塔基州的参议院席位空缺法，即如果参议院席位突然空缺，你需要做什么？嗯，会举行一场特别选举。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The governor has to call for it. And the seat has to be empty until a winner is certified. So, the idea is that the clock is somehow ticking on if, suddenly. there's an open seat in Kentucky, whether it can be filled. What I don't know is why Republicans are kind of leading the charge online, talking about this. This is Nancy Mace in a social media post: "If McConnell is in as bad a shape as Biden ever was, or worse, he needs to step aside. This charade can't continue.
paragraphs.cn: 州长必须召集选举。在获胜者得到确认之前，该席位必须保持空缺。所以，想法是，如果肯塔基州突然出现一个空缺席位，时间在滴答作响，看它能否被填补。我不知道的是，为什么共和党人在网上带头谈论这件事。这是南希·梅斯在社交媒体上发的帖子："如果麦康奈尔的状态和拜登一样糟糕，甚至更糟，他就应该让位。这场闹剧不能再继续下去了。"

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We can't demand of others what we won't demand of ourselves."
paragraphs.cn: "我们不能要求别人做到我们自己都做不到的事情。"

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.en: I think this is all getting kind of crazy, frankly. I can speak a little bit to this personally. When my dad was running for Congress in Iowa, Western Iowa, in 2020, he was diagnosed with stage four intestinal cancer. And we made the decision that, since it was an ongoing election, to come public and disclose everything. The reality is, is that I think McConnell is doing fine. Scott Jennings has backed up that he's completely fine, and he's coherent.
paragraphs.cn: 坦率地说，我认为这一切都变得有点疯狂了。我可以稍微谈一点个人经历。2020年，当我父亲在爱荷华州西部竞选国会议员时，他被诊断出患有四期肠癌。我们决定，由于选举正在进行，我们公开并披露了一切。事实是，我认为麦康奈尔做得很好。斯科特·詹宁斯证实他完全没问题，而且他思路清晰。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But don't skip over that. You said, "We made the decision to be public."
paragraphs.cn: 但别跳过这一点。你说，"我们决定公开。"

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Because there was a pending election that voters were going to make a decision on. But you look at what's happening in the Senate right now. They're not doing anything of substance. There's -- there's not going to be a SAVE Vote Act. It looks like they don't need his vote right now. He's announced his retirement. He's being replaced by, most likely, Andy Barr in Kentucky. It will be exciting to see. But I think this is much ado about nothing. I think we should let -- give him the grace and give him some privacy.
paragraphs.cn: 因为当时有一场即将到来的选举，选民们要做出决定。但看看现在参议院正在发生的事情。他们没有做任何实质性的事情。不会有《SAVE投票法案》。看起来他们现在不需要他的投票。他已经宣布退休。他很可能会被肯塔基州的安迪·巴尔取代。这将令人兴奋地看到。但我认为这完全是小题大做。我认为我们应该给予他宽容和隐私。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: He'll come out when he is -- when he feels comfortable.
paragraphs.cn: 他会在感到舒适的时候出来。

paragraphs.speaker: Elliot Williams
paragraphs.en: Can I ask a question? If you're a resident of the state of Kentucky and need help getting a passport with the State Department, who do you call?
paragraphs.cn: 我能问个问题吗？如果你是肯塔基州的居民，需要就护照问题联系国务院，你会打电话给谁？

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: You call Senator Mitch McConnell's office. You -- oh, please, the Senate staff. Everyone knows that the Senate staff is really where the magic happens and makes it all work.
paragraphs.cn: 你会打给参议员米奇·麦康奈尔的办公室。你 -- -- 哦，拜托，是参议院工作人员。每个人都知道，参议院工作人员才是真正创造奇迹、让一切运转的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: I think. Well, no, no, but I just -- I just think the notion that, because there is not a major piece of --
paragraphs.cn: 我想。嗯，不，不，但我只是 -- -- 我只是认为，因为没有一项重大法案 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Fighting legislation.
paragraphs.cn: 对抗立法。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Fighting legislation that's a priority to Republicans, that somehow, the residents of the state of Kentucky aren't entitled to -- aren't entitled to know, No. 1, what the health status is of one of the most senior senators in the United States.
paragraphs.cn: 对抗共和党优先考虑的立法，不知何故，肯塔基州居民无权知道 -- -- 首先，无权知道美国最资深参议员之一的健康状况。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: And two, the fact that there isn't an election somehow obviates the senator from one transparency or to doing his job.
paragraphs.cn: 其次，没有选举这一事实不知何故免除了参议员的透明度或履行其职责。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: There's probably a trust issue, too, because I think during -- when McConnell was a leader, he was someone who manipulated the calendar a lot, somebody who would delay votes for this reason or that reason, most famously, when it came to the Supreme Court. Meghan McCain weighed in, as well. Another child of a politico, saying, "I also have a lot of regrets almost a decade since my dad's passing.
paragraphs.cn: 可能还存在信任问题，因为我认为在麦康奈尔担任领袖期间，他经常操纵日程，以这样或那样的理由推迟投票，最著名的是在最高法院问题上。梅根·麦凯恩也发表了看法。另一位政治人物的孩子说："我父亲去世近十年来，我也有很多遗憾。我的家人本应在他确诊的那一刻就强迫他下台，这就是为什么我现在主张政治中的年龄和任期限制。我也真诚希望麦康奈尔参议员没事。"让我印象深刻的是"强迫"这个词。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: My family should have forced him to step down the second he was diagnosed, which is why I'm now an advocate of age and term limits in politics. I also sincerely hope Senator McConnell, OK." What struck me there was the word "forced."
paragraphs.cn: 是的。这是两件事。你既有政治事务又有个人事务的经历。作为个人事务，每个人都曾处理过年迈的父母，你必须拿走他们的车钥匙 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Yes. This is two things. And you have the experience of having it be both a political matter and a personal matter. Everyone, as a personal matter, has had to deal with aging parents who you have to take their car keys away and --
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 并进行那场艰难的对话。他们必须决定自己将做什么，比如是否要进入特殊设施等等。当这不仅仅是选举问题，而是透明度问题时，它就变成了政治事务。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: -- and have that difficult conversation. They have to decide what they're going to do when they -- you know, if they're going to go into special facilities, et cetera, et cetera. When it becomes more than that, it's when it's a political matter and not just about the election, but about transparency.
paragraphs.cn: 我确实接受这一点，此时，如果米奇·麦康奈尔身体状况良好，足以和斯科特·詹宁斯闲聊，那么他也应该能够打电话给CNN或《纽约时报》或福克斯新闻等，以表明他没事。而他没有这样做，坦率地说，这导致了这些阴谋论。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And I do take the point that, at this point, if -- if Mitch McConnell is well enough to be gabbing to Scott Jennings, he should be well enough to call in to CNN or to "The New York Times" or to FOX News or whatever, to -- to show that he's OK. And the fact that he hasn't done that is quite -- quite frankly, leading to these conspiracies.
paragraphs.cn: 如果我可以的话，因为我认为有一种善意的解读方式，特别是如果你了解米奇·麦康奈尔。他是一个非常坚强的人。他不喜欢被告知该做什么，尤其是不喜欢来自公众压力的指示。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: If I could, because, again, I think there's a charitable way to read this, especially if you know Mitch McConnell. He is a very strong man. He does not like being told what to do, especially by public pressure.
paragraphs.cn: 他按照自己的时间表行事。所以，我认为你应该给他时间。给他几天时间，深呼吸一下。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: He -- he works on his own time frame. And so, I think you've got to give it to him. Give him a few days, take it -- take a deep breath.

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But let me play for someone you pointed out this week who you could describe very similarly, the former defense secretary, Lloyd Austin. When he went to the hospital for his treatment, it became quite the scandal for Republicans. And in the end, he came out, more or less kind of apologized. And I want to play for you the lesson that he took away from that moment.
paragraphs.cn: 但让我为你播放一段关于你本周提到的一个人的内容，你也可以用非常相似的方式描述他，那就是前国防部长劳埃德·奥斯汀。当他去医院接受治疗时，这件事在共和党人中引起了不小的丑闻。最后，他站出来，或多或少地道歉了。我想让你听听他从那一刻吸取的教训。

paragraphs.speaker: Lloyd Austin
paragraphs.en: I don't think it's news that I'm a pretty private guy. I never liked burdening others with my problems. It's just not my way. But I've learned from this experience, taking this kind of job means losing some of the privacy that most of us expect. The American people have a right to know if their leaders are facing health challenges that might affect their ability to perform their duties, even temporarily.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为我相当注重隐私，这不算新闻。我从来不喜欢用自己的问题给别人添麻烦。这不是我的风格。但通过这次经历，我学到，担任这样的工作意味着要失去我们大多数人期望的某些隐私。美国人民有权知道他们的领导人是否面临可能影响其履行职责能力的健康挑战，即使是暂时的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: "Even temporarily." Also notice, he said, the loss of privacy is part of the gig. And I just want to put up some images here, in memoriam. These are lawmakers who have died while they were holding office since 2024. And you pointed out many of these names.
paragraphs.cn: "即使是暂时的。" 还要注意，他说失去隐私是这份工作的一部分。我只想在这里展示一些图片，以示纪念。这些是自2024年以来在任期间去世的议员。你提到了其中许多名字。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: A lot of Dems up there.
paragraphs.cn: 上面有很多民主党人。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Well, it's a lot of old people.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，有很多老年人。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: I mean, I think let's just -- We should also discuss the fact that the United States Senate is a gerontocracy. It is. Well, it is.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，我认为我们应该讨论一下美国参议院是一个老人政治的事实。确实如此。嗯，确实如此。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I think the reason why people are discussing is because the Biden thing --
paragraphs.cn: 我认为人们讨论的原因是因为拜登的事情 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- was like a big wakeup call where everybody was questioning, and Republicans are still questioning.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 就像一个巨大的警钟，每个人都在质疑，而共和党人仍在质疑。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Can we talk about the Trump thing? I mean, this is the other thing.
paragraphs.cn: 我们能谈谈特朗普的事情吗？我的意思是，这是另一回事。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: We don't know what President Trump actually is suffering from. What are his swelling --
paragraphs.cn: 我们不知道特朗普总统实际上患有什么病。他的肿胀是什么 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: What are you talking about?
paragraphs.cn: 你在说什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Well, there's a lot of --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，有很多 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: There's -- we have done many segments on the rumors about various health issues.
paragraphs.cn: 有 -- -- 我们做过很多关于各种健康问题传闻的报道。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: About various health issues where doctors speculated, with has swollen ankles, you know, the continued bruising on his arms, like -- on his hands. I mean, there are lots of questions about, when -- when people get to a certain age. I mean, let's be honest. I mean, he's 80. Mitch McConnell is -- how old is Mitch McConnell now?
paragraphs.cn: 关于各种健康问题，医生们猜测，比如脚踝肿胀，还有他手臂上持续的瘀伤，手上也是。我的意思是，当人们到了某个年龄，会有很多疑问。说实话，他80岁了。米奇·麦康奈尔 -- -- 米奇·麦康奈尔现在多大了？

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: He should be in his 80s. I don't think --
paragraphs.cn: 他应该80多岁了。我不认为 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Yes, he's in his 80s. I mean, obviously, Biden was also in his 80s. I mean, you start to have serious health concerns. And my point about this, more broadly, is I think the more general point, which is about transparency and what do you owe the voting public about --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，他80多岁了。显然，拜登也是80多岁。我是说，你会开始有严重的健康担忧。我更广泛的观点是，我认为更普遍的问题是透明度，以及你对投票公众应该交代什么 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: What do you owe the voting public about your health?
paragraphs.cn: 关于你的健康，你对投票公众应该交代什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And here's a fact I owe all of you. He's 84.
paragraphs.cn: 而我要告诉你们一个事实：他84岁了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But I have to be honest. One of the things that happened with Biden and Trump was this idea of, like, well, there's a vigorous and, you know, still operating 80-something. And then there's, like, a deteriorating something. And I think what the audience and voters want to know is, are we all on the same page about when it's OK not to say anything --
paragraphs.cn: 但老实说，拜登和特朗普身上发生的一件事是，有一种观点认为，有些80多岁的人精力充沛、仍在运作，而另一些则在衰退。我认为观众和选民想知道的是，我们是否都同意什么时候可以不说什么 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: -- about what's happening? And the answer is sounding more and more like no.
paragraphs.cn: -- -- 关于正在发生的事情？而答案越来越像是：不。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Well, yes. And moreover, I think a lot of people showed their behinds about Bidens age. I mean, I think we went through -- people don't remember, there was a year-long debate about Joe Biden being unfit for the presidency.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，是的。而且，我认为很多人在拜登的年龄问题上暴露了自己的无知。我的意思是，我们经历了一场长达一年的关于乔·拜登是否适合担任总统的辩论。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Which was right for us to have as a nation, because it was clear the president was not fit. This is an individual who is, No. 1, one of the senior leaders on the Appropriations Committee, a former Senate majority leader and a representative serving the state of Kentucky. And I think it's a reasonable question to ask, is this individual fit to serve? And are all the people who are out there vouching for his health creating problems for themselves?
paragraphs.cn: 作为一个国家，我们进行这样的辩论是正确的，因为很明显总统并不适合。这个人，首先，是拨款委员会的高级领导人之一，前参议院多数党领袖，也是肯塔基州的众议员。我认为问这样一个问题是合理的：这个人是否适合任职？所有那些为他健康背书的人，是否在给自己制造麻烦？

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Later on, by being on the record about it?
paragraphs.cn: 后来，通过公开记录这些言论？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Let me follow up on something else quite serious. Coming up on CNN THIS MORNING, is Iran looking to assassinate President Trump? This was the warning from Israel to tip off the White House. Plus, extreme heat fueling dangerous and deadly wildfires in Spain. And where will LeBron go? The odds on Kalshi say there's a greater than 60 percent chance he heads to Cleveland, followed by the Heat and Warriors. We're going to tell you what Charles Barkley thinks.
paragraphs.cn: 让我跟进另一个非常严肃的话题。在CNN《今晨》节目中，伊朗是否在密谋暗杀特朗普总统？这是以色列向白宫发出的警告。此外，极端高温在西班牙引发了危险且致命的野火。还有，勒布朗会去哪里？Kalshi的赔率显示，他有超过60%的可能性去克利夫兰，其次是热火和勇士。我们将告诉你查尔斯·巴克利是怎么想的。

paragraphs.speaker: Charles Barkley
paragraphs.en: He should go back to Cleveland and finish his career there. If he goes to Philly, there's people going to say he's ring-chasing to catch Michael. If he goes to Golden State, they're going to say he's ring- chasing.
paragraphs.cn: 他应该回到克利夫兰并在那里结束职业生涯。如果他去费城，会有人说他为了追赶迈克尔而追逐戒指。如果他去金州，他们也会说他是在追逐戒指。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, an alleged plot by Iran to assassinate President Trump, uncovered by Israel. Sources tell CNN they shared the intelligence with the United States this week.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，伊朗涉嫌密谋暗杀特朗普总统的计划被以色列发现。消息人士告诉CNN，他们本周与美国分享了这一情报。

paragraphs.speaker: Donald Trump
paragraphs.en: I'm No. 1 on the kill list for Iran. They're lovely people. I'm No. 1. I may be gone, too, because I'm their No. 1 target. It's out all over the place. I'm their No. 1, because they're scum.
paragraphs.cn: 我是伊朗暗杀名单上的头号目标。他们是一群可爱的人。我是头号目标。我也可能不在了，因为我是他们的头号目标。这到处都是。我是他们的头号目标，因为他们是人渣。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, during funeral processions for the former Iranian leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, a hardline Iranian newspaper put a $100 million bounty on Trump's head. And there were countless banners calling for the president to be assassinated, along with other U.S. politicos like Senator Lindsey Graham. Graham posted this photo on social media, remarking, "At least they used a good photo of me." Joining the group chat, Holly Dagres, senior fellow at the Washington Institute for Near East Policy. Obviously, the U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，在伊朗前领导人阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊的葬礼游行期间，一家伊朗强硬派报纸悬赏1亿美元要特朗普的人头。还有无数横幅呼吁暗杀总统以及其他美国政界人士，比如参议员林赛·格雷厄姆。格雷厄姆在社交媒体上发布了这张照片，并评论道："至少他们用了我一张好照片。"加入群聊的是华盛顿近东政策研究所高级研究员霍莉·达格雷斯。显然，美国

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: and Israel were able to take out whole tiers of leadership in Iran. So, talk about how serious this assassination threat is, could have been based on what you're reading on the sources.
paragraphs.cn: 和以色列能够消灭伊朗的多层领导层。那么，谈谈这个暗杀威胁有多严重，可能基于你从消息来源读到的信息。

paragraphs.speaker: Holly Dagres
paragraphs.en: Well, first, we should start by saying that this was related to the targeted killing of Quds Force Commander Qasem Soleimani by a U.S. drone strike in Baghdad in 2020. So, these calls for an assassination of not just President Donald Trump, but senior U.S. officials that were in the first Trump administration: then-Secretary of State Mike Pompeo, national security adviser John Bolton, among those. And actually, there have been two plots that were foiled related to the latter two. So, this isn't entirely new.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，首先，我们应该指出，这与2020年美国在巴格达用无人机袭击暗杀圣城旅指挥官卡西姆·苏莱曼尼有关。因此，这些呼吁不仅针对唐纳德·特朗普总统，还包括第一届特朗普政府中的高级美国官员：时任国务卿迈克·蓬佩奥、国家安全顾问约翰·博尔顿等人。实际上，针对后两人已经挫败了两起阴谋。所以，这并不完全是新鲜事。

paragraphs.speaker: Holly Dagres
paragraphs.en: But to your point, I mean, this is right now happening after a three-month war.
paragraphs.cn: 但就你的观点而言，我的意思是，这是在三个月的战争之后发生的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I do want to raise something, which is that the people who have been very critical of this war and critical of the U.S. and the Netanyahu and Trump relationship, have kind of questioned intel from Netanyahu, for example, on nuclear breakout periods or something like that. I want to play for you. This is Hillary Clinton. This is many weeks back, but she was talking about what it was like to deal with Netanyahu when she was secretary of state. And it's relevant here. Let me play it for you.
paragraphs.cn: 我确实想提一件事，那就是那些对这场战争持批评态度、对美国以及内塔尼亚胡和特朗普关系持批评态度的人，对内塔尼亚胡的情报提出过质疑，比如关于核突破时间之类的情报。我想给你放一段录音。这是希拉里·克林顿，几周前的，她当时在谈论她担任国务卿时与内塔尼亚胡打交道的经历。这与此相关。让我放给你听。

paragraphs.speaker: Hillary Clinton
paragraphs.en: I remember one day I was on the phone for hours with Ehud, with Bibi, with others, you know. And they would say things like, you know, our planes are on the tarmac. And I'd say, Well, good luck. I mean, great. Why are you doing this?
paragraphs.cn: 我记得有一天，我和埃胡德、比比还有其他人通了几个小时电话。他们会说，我们的飞机已经在停机坪上了。我会说，祝你好运。我是说，很好。你们为什么要这么做？

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: You're saying you were -- they were -- you were -- you were being played?
paragraphs.cn: 你是说你在 -- -- 他们 -- -- 你 -- -- 你被耍了？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Can you talk about this in this context? I think where people are speaking about Netanyahu and Israel with suspicion.
paragraphs.cn: 你能在这个背景下谈谈吗？我认为现在人们正带着怀疑谈论内塔尼亚胡和以色列。

paragraphs.speaker: Dagres
paragraphs.en: Well, we've seen incidents where the Israeli prime minister, Benjamin Netanyahu, has been speaking to an audience of one, which famously in 2018, right before President Donald Trump withdrew from the Iran nuclear deal, Netanyahu did this very well-known presentation in English, of course, about new findings that Mossad had found from some files they had stolen from an undeclared site.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，我们见过一些事件，以色列总理本雅明·内塔尼亚胡曾向单一听众发表讲话，最著名的是2018年，就在唐纳德·特朗普总统退出伊朗核协议之前，内塔尼亚胡用英语做了一个非常著名的演示，关于摩萨德从一个未申报地点偷来的文件中发现的新发现。

paragraphs.speaker: Dagres
paragraphs.en: But even though it wasn't exactly new information, the fact that this had been presented was really what sold President Trump to decide that he was finally going to withdraw from the nuclear deal in May of 2018. So, I think that a lot of questions are arising in this moment, because it's clear that the goals that the Israelis had for the war hadn't been met, but had to be deterred because of the ceasefire. And there's an understanding that there's still unfinished business with Tehran.
paragraphs.cn: 但尽管这并非全新信息，但这一演示的呈现方式确实说服了特朗普总统最终决定在2018年5月退出核协议。所以，我认为此刻出现了很多问题，因为很明显，以色列在战争中的目标尚未实现，但由于停火而不得不中止。而且人们明白，与德黑兰之间仍有未完成的事务。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I know it's been actually hard for you, because you have a lot of family and connection to Iran. We've been seeing these images of people mourning the death of the supreme leader. But this is a dictatorship with -- that had a severe and violent crackdown of protests. What is the feeling for people who didn't have to make their way out into those streets?
paragraphs.cn: 我知道这对你来说其实很难，因为你有很多家人和伊朗有联系。我们看到了人们哀悼最高领袖去世的画面。但这是一个独裁政权，曾对抗议活动进行过严厉和暴力的镇压。对于那些不必走上街头的人来说，感受如何？

paragraphs.speaker: Dagres
paragraphs.en: Well, you know, ironically, this past week marked the six- month anniversary of the January anti-regime uprising that resulted in the deaths of thousands of protesters. And so, a lot of those Iranians were honoring their memory, the people that could afford to in the capital of Tehran, where much of the main procession was happening for Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei's death was -- people took it as an opportunity to leave, to go to the North or Caspian Sea. And so, those people were not on the streets.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，讽刺的是，上周正好是1月份反政权起义六个月的纪念日，那次起义导致数千名抗议者死亡。所以，很多伊朗人在纪念他们，那些有能力的人在首都德黑兰，那里是最高领袖阿亚图拉·阿里·哈梅内伊葬礼的主要游行地点 -- -- 人们借此机会离开，前往北部或里海。所以，那些人没有上街。

paragraphs.speaker: Dagres
paragraphs.en: They were not. And if you were an anti-regime protests or lost a loved one, I remember some of the commentary after the Internet blackout ended was, oh, well, you didn't let us bury our loved ones, but it took you many months to bury your own, meaning the supreme leader. So, I think that really tells a lot about this moment.
paragraphs.cn: 他们没有。如果你是一名反政权抗议者或失去了亲人，我记得网络中断结束后的一些评论是，哦，你们不让我们埋葬亲人，但你们却花了几个月才埋葬自己的，指的是最高领袖。所以，我认为这很能说明此刻的情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Are people still scared?
paragraphs.cn: 人们还在害怕吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Dagres
paragraphs.en: They're scared for many reasons, Audie. They're scared because they don't know where their future is, because they don't -- if you're an anti-regime Iranian, you don't want this regime. And you had the United States arguably say that help was on the way. That is, President Donald Trump, and that this was a regime change, or at least initially. And then to see that this is a more emboldened regime, one that, surprisingly, has more support from people that are critics of the war.
paragraphs.cn: 他们害怕的原因有很多，Audie。他们害怕是因为不知道未来在哪里，因为 -- -- 如果你是反政权的伊朗人，你不想要这个政权。而且美国可以说曾表示援助即将到来。也就是说，唐纳德·特朗普总统曾表示这是一场政权更迭，至少最初是这样。然后看到这个政权更加胆大妄为，令人惊讶的是，它得到了更多批评战争的人的支持。

paragraphs.speaker: Dagres
paragraphs.en: And -- but also there's a worry: like, what if this war does continue? Their lives have gotten effectively worse. Economy's in shambles. They're saying they haven't seen numbers like that since the Iran-Iraq war. And so, if you're a young Iranian, you feel like your future -- there isn't really one. So, this is where Iranians are feeling, at least in this moment. I would say.
paragraphs.cn: 而且 -- -- 但也有一种担忧：比如，如果这场战争真的继续下去呢？他们的生活实际上变得更糟了。经济一片混乱。他们说自两伊战争以来从未见过这样的数字。所以，如果你是一个年轻的伊朗人，你会觉得你的未来 -- -- 其实没有未来。所以，这就是伊朗人现在的感受，至少在这一刻。我想说。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK. Holly, thank you so much for talking to us in this inflection point. We'll have you back soon.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，Holly，非常感谢你在这个转折点与我们交谈。我们很快会再请你来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, after the break on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to talk about the worker who saved a New York City high rise from collapse. They're speaking out.
paragraphs.cn: 现在，在CNN今晨节目休息之后，我们将讨论那位拯救了纽约市一座高层建筑免于倒塌的工人。他们正在发声。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: In this situation. They're calling me a hero. I don't believe I am.
paragraphs.cn: 在这种情况下。他们称我为英雄。我不认为我是。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The job isn't done yet, however. Can workers save the building from partial demolition? Plus, Meta bolsters privacy features on its secret recording Ray-Ban glasses. Will that make them any less creepy? And a live look at the White House. The president's next construction project, the White House columns. You can see already they are covered with scaffolding.
paragraphs.cn: 然而，工作还没有完成。工人们能拯救这座建筑免于部分拆除吗？另外，Meta加强了其秘密录制Ray-Ban眼镜的隐私功能。这会让它们不那么令人毛骨悚然吗？还有白宫的实时画面。总统的下一个建设项目，白宫柱子。你可以看到它们已经被脚手架覆盖了。

paragraphs.speaker: Kylie Jenner
paragraphs.en: Hi, I'm Kylie Jenner, the new voice of Meta A.I. Hey, Meta A.I., is that a chicken?
paragraphs.cn: 嗨，我是Kylie Jenner，Meta人工智能的新声音。嘿，Meta人工智能，那是一只鸡吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, Kylie Jenner. As she says, the new face and, it turns out, voice of Meta A.I. glasses. But, as everything with the Kardashians and A.I., comes the backlash.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，Kylie Jenner。正如她所说，Meta人工智能眼镜的新面孔，结果也是新声音。但是，就像卡戴珊家族和人工智能的一切一样，随之而来的是反弹。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: There's a way for you to tell, usually, if somebody is filming on their phone. With the glasses, there is no way for you to tell. And they are trying to get women more and more comfortable with that. So, enter Kylie Jenner with this very cute pair of glasses.
paragraphs.cn: 通常有一种方法可以判断是否有人在用手机拍摄。但有了这副眼镜，你无法判断。他们正试图让女性越来越适应这一点。所以，Kylie Jenner带着这副非常可爱的眼镜登场了。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Female
paragraphs.en: This collaboration with Kylie ultimately is roping in women consumers.
paragraphs.cn: 与凯莉的合作最终是为了吸引女性消费者。

paragraphs.speaker: Unidentified Male
paragraphs.en: To me, it's almost as if Meta is saying, like, OK, these creepy men are recording you, but hey, you can record them right back.
paragraphs.cn: 在我看来，这几乎就像Meta在说，好吧，那些 creepy 的男人在录你，但嘿，你也可以录他们。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: All right. Needless to say, seems to be some real concerns. Turns out users can actually tamper with that LED light on the glasses, so you wouldn't know if you were being recorded. So, now Meta is trying to address some of those concerns, saying it's rolling out a new update that will turn off the camera if users turn off the privacy light.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。不用说，似乎确实存在一些真正的担忧。事实证明，用户可以篡改眼镜上的LED灯，所以你无法知道自己是否被录制。现在，Meta正试图解决其中一些问题，称正在推出新更新，如果用户关闭隐私灯，摄像头也会关闭。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, we're talking about all of this on this week's "Engagement Party." So, joining the group chat, Ari Shapiro, my co-host of "Engagement Party." So, we were joking about the various tiers of people who hate this. ARI SHAPIRO, CO-HOST, "ENGAGEMENT PARTY": Yes. This intersects with a lot of different backlashes.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我们在本周的《订婚派对》中讨论这一切。加入群聊的是我的联合主持人阿里·夏皮罗。我们开玩笑说，讨厌这个的人分不同层次。阿里·夏皮罗（《订婚派对》联合主持人）：是的。这涉及很多不同的反对声音。

paragraphs.speaker: Shapiro
paragraphs.en: All going on at once.
paragraphs.cn: 所有反对声音同时出现。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But also, there are some people who might make use of them. Help me understand why it's such a big thing online.
paragraphs.cn: 但也有一些可能会使用它们的人。帮我理解为什么这在网上引起这么大的反响。

paragraphs.speaker: Shapiro
paragraphs.en: All right. Well, first of all, you've got the A.I. backlash. You've got the tech billionaires backlash. You've got the surveillance technology backlash. So, this is kind of summed up by people who are online calling this either Cybertrucks for the face, or there was another line I saw that: was the biggest advance in pervert technology since the trench coat.
paragraphs.cn: 好吧。首先，有对人工智能的反对。有对科技亿万富翁的反对。有对监控技术的反对。所以，这基本上被网上的人总结为要么是"脸上的赛博卡车"，要么是我看到的另一句话：自风衣以来变态技术的最大进步。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Shapiro
paragraphs.en: I think Meta has a public history of, No. 1, compromising their users' safety and security and privacy, then publicly apologizing for it. And No. 2, sinking tens of billions of dollars into transformative technologies that then die, like the Metaverse. Too early to say if this is going to be one or both of those things, but the public response is, to say the least, not positive.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为Meta有公开的历史：第一，损害用户的安全和隐私，然后公开道歉。第二，投入数百亿美元于变革性技术，然后这些技术消亡，比如元宇宙。现在说这会是其中之一还是两者兼有还为时过早，但公众反应至少可以说是不积极的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. And we should say what started out kind of like social media stuff here and there, especially being posted by women, has moved to the lawsuit face [SIC] -- phase of this with you've got some states -- let me see who it was. There's some kid safety lawsuits that they're dealing with, four state attorneys general seeking $1.4 trillion in damages. And there have been a couple other cases, as you've mentioned, that Meta has either lost or settled this year.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。我们应该说，最初只是社交媒体上的一些内容，尤其是女性发布的，现在已经进入诉讼阶段 -- -- 一些州 -- -- 让我看看是谁。他们正在处理一些儿童安全诉讼，四个州的检察长要求赔偿1.4万亿美元。还有另外几个案件，正如你提到的，Meta今年要么败诉要么和解了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And then there's just, Lulu, people like you who have encountered these in the wild.
paragraphs.cn: 然后还有像你这样的人，在现实生活中遇到过这些眼镜。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I've encountered these in the wild. It was deeply uncomfortable. Someone was, I thought, filming me when I was with my daughter. I didn't like it. And then it just starts to --
paragraphs.cn: 我在现实生活中遇到过这种情况。非常不舒服。我以为有人在拍我和我女儿。我不喜欢这样。然后就开始 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: So, I was in a public space. But at the same time, it leads to these very awkward interactions. What are the social niceties for me to say, like, hello, sir, I don't -- are you filming me with your Meta glasses? I don't want to be filmed."
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我当时在公共场合。但同时，这导致了非常尴尬的互动。我该说什么社交礼仪呢，比如，你好，先生，我不 -- -- 你是不是在用你的Meta眼镜拍我？我不想被拍。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: No. It was just like this incredibly awkward. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 不。就是非常尴尬。

paragraphs.speaker: Lulu Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I don't -- are you filming me you're your Meta glasses? I don't want to be filmed. ARI SHAPIRO, CO-HOST, "ENGAGEMENT PARTY"
paragraphs.cn: 我不 -- -- 你是不是在用你的Meta眼镜拍我？我不想被拍。阿里·夏皮罗，联合主持人，"订婚派对"

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: No. It was just like this incredibly awkward interaction where he just, like, kind of glared at me, took off his glasses, and, you know, it was just this very weird -- as a, you know, we gotcha.
paragraphs.cn: 不。就是一次非常尴尬的互动，他只是瞪了我一眼，摘下眼镜，你知道，就是非常奇怪 -- -- 就像，我们抓到你了。

paragraphs.speaker: Audie Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes. Like, I'm not filming you, but I'm taking these off. Yes.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。就像，我没在拍你，但我把眼镜摘下来了。是的。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Yes. And so the whole thing was just really uncomfortable. And I don't, you know, and I don't know what the social interactions are. I put -- I posted this on social media. I got all these responses basically saying, it's legal to film people in most states, which I think you can speak to.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。所以整件事非常不舒服。我不知道社交互动该怎样。我把这件事发到了社交媒体上，收到了很多回复，基本上都说在大多数州拍摄他人是合法的，我想你可以谈谈这个。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: What's the deal? What can we do?
paragraphs.cn: 怎么回事？我们能做什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: It really is legal to film people in most states. Think about every time you go to a monument or a park, you see someone out with their cell phone. Well, they're filming you too. You're incidentally in the camera. Now, it gets trickier where you are recording one person. Some states you have to -- both people have to know that they're being -- that someone's being recorded.
paragraphs.cn: 在大多数州拍摄他人确实是合法的。想想每次你去纪念碑或公园，都会看到有人拿着手机。他们也在拍你。你只是偶然出现在镜头里。但如果你专门拍摄一个人，情况就更复杂了。有些州要求双方都知道 -- -- 有人正在被录音。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Some states you can just record one person. One interesting thing that Ari had said about Meta being sued or just taking fire over the years over its users privacy --
paragraphs.cn: 有些州你可以只录一个人。阿里提到Meta多年来因用户隐私被起诉或受到抨击，这很有趣 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Shapiro
paragraphs.en: Especially for Instagram.
paragraphs.cn: 尤其是对Instagram而言。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Especially for Instagram. Here -- this is interesting because it's not just users' privacy, it's third party's privacies. People like Lulu who are now at -- or anybody now at risk. It's not just the user who's getting pulled into this, right? And so, even if someone's not being filmed, there's just this specter of --
paragraphs.cn: 尤其是对Instagram而言。这里 -- -- 这很有趣，因为这不仅涉及用户的隐私，还涉及第三方的隐私。像Lulu这样的人现在处于 -- -- 或者说任何人现在都处于风险之中。被卷入其中的不仅仅是用户，对吧？所以，即使某人没有被拍摄，也存在这种阴影 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: At any point I might be filmed, I don't know if I'm being filmed, and that's deeply uncomfortable.
paragraphs.cn: 任何时候我都有可能被拍摄，我不知道自己是否正在被拍摄，这让人深感不安。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: To admit (ph) to people.
paragraphs.cn: 向人们承认这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And we talked about the potential uses for this.
paragraphs.cn: 我们讨论过这项技术的潜在用途。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: In the accessibility community. People who are hard of hearing. People who have issues with their site. The information they'd be able to get in the glasses. I'm pretty sure I can guess lawmakers -- law enforcement is going to be excited about this. So, there are uses for it. And it's interesting that the way they're marketing it though is like, hey girls, this is fun.
paragraphs.cn: 在无障碍社区中。听力有障碍的人。视力有问题的人。他们能够通过眼镜获取信息。我很确定我能猜到立法者 -- -- 执法部门会对这个感到兴奋。所以，它是有用途的。有趣的是，他们的营销方式却是：嘿，姑娘们，这很有趣。

paragraphs.speaker: Shapiro
paragraphs.en: Oh, they've got this lineup of mega celebs. I mean they're spending, I think, $70 billion to promote and produce these.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，他们请来了众多超级巨星。我的意思是，他们花了大概700亿美元来推广和生产这些产品。

paragraphs.speaker: Shapiro
paragraphs.en: And they've got Teyana Taylor, Doja Cat, Kylie Jenner. Like, they are bringing out the all-stars to try to tell people that this is cool. I feel like it's part of Mark Zuckerberg's long campaign to convince people that he's cool. He wanted to be the Elon Musk pre- DOGE, and maybe his reputation is heading more in a direction of Elon Musk today.
paragraphs.cn: 他们请来了Teyana Taylor、Doja Cat、Kylie Jenner。他们请来了全明星阵容，试图告诉人们这很酷。我觉得这是马克·扎克伯格长期努力的一部分，试图让人们相信他很酷。他想成为DOGE之前的埃隆·马斯克，也许如今他的声誉正朝着埃隆·马斯克的方向发展。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Even if he's in the front row of Prada at the start of their relationship?
paragraphs.cn: 即使在他们关系刚开始时，他就坐在普拉达秀场的前排？

paragraphs.speaker: Shapiro
paragraphs.en: Yes, I'm not sure that that's a sign.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，我不确定那是一个信号。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: But it's all -- but at the end of the day, nobody really trusts Meta with their own personal safety online.
paragraphs.cn: 但归根结底，没有人真正信任Meta能保障他们在网上的个人安全。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: The data that's ours.
paragraphs.cn: 数据是我们的。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: The data that's ours. I mean right now there's a whole kerfuffle over Instagram. If you have a public facing Instagram page, Meta is now automatically opting you in to basically allowing your public Instagram page to be manipulated by A.I. You have to actually actively go in there, which I did yesterday, to change those settings. These are the kinds of actions that make people very upset.
paragraphs.cn: 数据是我们的。我的意思是，现在Instagram上就有一场轩然大波。如果你有一个公开的Instagram页面，Meta现在会自动让你选择允许你的公开Instagram页面被人工智能操控。你必须主动进入设置，我昨天就这么做了，去更改这些设置。这类行为让人们非常不满。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: CAA is getting involved in this, of course, the big Hollywood agency. These are issues that really come at the core of people's identity, their public facing profiles.
paragraphs.cn: 当然，大型好莱坞经纪公司CAA也参与其中。这些问题确实触及了人们身份认同和公开形象的核心。

paragraphs.speaker: Shapiro
paragraphs.en: So, I think what we're seeing here, as I say in "Engagement Party," in our latest episode, is, number one, a failure of government to actually protect people's privacy and identity. But number two, a success of the people who are saying, you may be spending tens of billions of dollars to make this a thing, but we don't want it to be a thing.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我认为我们在这里看到的，正如我在最新一集《参与派对》中所说，第一，是政府未能真正保护人们的隐私和身份。但第二，是那些说"你们可能花费数百亿美元来推行这件事，但我们不希望它成为现实"的人取得了成功。

paragraphs.speaker: Shapiro
paragraphs.en: And the people have the final say.
paragraphs.cn: 人民拥有最终决定权。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: What do the young say, it's propaganda they're unsubscribing from? Ari Shapiro, thank you so much. You can catch us on the newest episode of "Engagement Party" now. New episodes are out Friday. We talk about this and also the movement of FROYO. And so, it's a lot of fun. Thanks so much, Ari. All right, straight ahead on CNN THIS MORNING, we're going to turn to this news. The threats from Mexico to take the U.S. to court over the deaths of ICE detainees. And could a foreign government succeed where U.S.
paragraphs.cn: 年轻人怎么说，他们正在取消订阅的宣传？阿里·夏皮罗，非常感谢你。你现在可以在最新一集的《参与派对》中看到我们。新剧集每周五播出。我们讨论了这个问题，还有FROYO运动。非常有趣。非常感谢，阿里。好的，接下来在《CNN今晨》节目中，我们将关注这条新闻：墨西哥威胁要将美国告上法庭，涉及ICE拘留者死亡事件。外国政府能否在美国倡导者失败的地方取得成功？

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: advocates have failed? Plus, personality over substance. Voters getting candidates who are actually qualified for the job. Maybe. Maybe not.
paragraphs.cn: 此外，个性重于实质。选民们得到了真正有资格胜任工作的候选人。也许吧。也许不是。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, the 52-year-old Mexican man killed by ICE in Houston this week was not the target of an immigration operation. That's according to a source. Texas authorities previously notified ICE about two people believed to be in the U.S. illegally, traveling in a white van. And neither of those men were the victim. You're looking at video from the aftermath of the shooting. I have to warn you, it is disturbing because you can see a bloodied person on the ground.
paragraphs.cn: 本周在休斯顿被ICE杀害的52岁墨西哥男子并非移民行动的目标。据消息人士称，得克萨斯州当局此前通知ICE，有两名据信非法滞留在美国的人乘坐一辆白色货车旅行。这两人都不是受害者。你看到的是枪击事件后的视频。我必须警告你，这令人不安，因为你可以看到地上有一个血迹斑斑的人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Now, there's no official footage of the shooting that has been released. Federal officials involved were not wearing body cameras. ICE claims the man attempted to take off as agents tried to pull him over. His family is pushing back on that.
paragraphs.cn: 目前还没有官方发布的枪击画面。涉案的联邦官员没有佩戴随身摄像头。ICE声称该男子在特工试图让他停车时试图逃跑。他的家人对此予以反驳。

paragraphs.speaker: Ronaldo Salgado
paragraphs.en: He did not know that those vehicles following him were ICE agents. Again, these vehicles were unmarked. They had no emblems, no logos, no flashing lights. There was no way for him to know that they were ICE agents. I know my dad would have stopped had ICE agents formally and clearly identified themselves, at least through their vehicle markings.
paragraphs.cn: 他不知道那些跟在他后面的车辆是ICE特工。同样，这些车没有标记。它们没有徽章、没有标志、没有闪光灯。他根本无法知道他们是ICE特工。我知道，如果ICE特工正式且明确地表明身份，至少通过车辆标识，我父亲会停车的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: In the meantime, Mexico, fed up with the deaths of its people on U.S. soil. Their president is seeking a criminal investigation in the U.S. related deaths of 17 Mexican nationals during immigration operations in recent months. PRESIDENT CLAUDIA SHEINBAUM, MEXICO (through translator): What happened recently with the death of a Mexican in an ICE operation, not only is it sad and regrettable, but it seems to be targeted. So, obviously, we will maintain the diplomatic relationship.
paragraphs.cn: 与此同时，墨西哥对其公民在美国领土上的死亡感到厌倦。墨西哥总统正寻求在美国对近几个月移民行动中17名墨西哥国民的死亡进行刑事调查。墨西哥总统克劳迪娅·辛鲍姆（通过翻译）表示：最近一名墨西哥人在ICE行动中死亡，不仅令人悲伤和遗憾，而且似乎是有针对性的。因此，显然我们将维持外交关系。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But we made the decision to file a formal complaint with the state and federal prosecutor's office of the United States against those who are found responsible for what we consider to be homicides.
paragraphs.cn: 但我们决定向美国州和联邦检察官办公室提出正式投诉，追究那些我们认为构成杀人罪的责任人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Mexico's foreign minister says the country will file criminal complaints directly with U.S. prosecutors. The group chat is back. And, Elliot, we need your expertise.
paragraphs.cn: 墨西哥外长表示，该国将直接向美国检察官提起刑事诉讼。群聊又回来了。埃利奥特，我们需要你的专业知识。

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Cornish
paragraphs.en: Because, in general, when you're looking at deaths of ICE detainees --
paragraphs.cn: 因为，总的来说，当你审视ICE被拘留者的死亡时 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Obviously there is a spike. As of 2025, that number up to 33. It's gone down a bit. And I think people thought maybe ICE had been operating differently, or at least more covertly on the streets of the
paragraphs.cn: 显然有一个高峰。截至2025年，这一数字上升到33。后来有所下降。我认为人们可能以为ICE的运作方式有所不同，或者至少在美国街头更加隐蔽。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You're shaking your head.
paragraphs.cn: 你在摇头。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: No. It's just covertly might be the word or just simply not in public in the same way. Now, the big picture governing all of this is that law enforcement has tremendously broad latitude. And I think latitude that would shock and alarm many people, both in terms of deaths in custody, but also in terms of their conduct in the field. Now, certainly, Mexico is free to try to, you know, engage prosecutors, file lawsuits or whatever else.
paragraphs.cn: 不。只是"隐蔽"这个词可能合适，或者干脆就是不像以前那样公开。现在，这一切的大背景是执法部门拥有极其广泛的自由裁量权。我认为这种自由裁量权会让很多人震惊和警觉，无论是在押人员死亡方面，还是他们在现场的行为方面。当然，墨西哥可以自由地尝试接触检察官、提起诉讼或其他任何方式。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: I will note, you know, the Department of Homeland Security will be defended by the Justice Department in any federal suit in the United States right now. And I just think the likelihood of any of these suits really gaining traction --
paragraphs.cn: 我要指出，目前在美国任何联邦诉讼中，国土安全部都将由司法部辩护。我只是认为这些诉讼取得进展的可能性 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Just based on who the Justice Department is right now, they have an interest in protecting the Department of Homeland Security, is pretty low, but they can. That is their right. But two other avenues. Really quick, Audie, two other avenues for proceeding, if they're serious. Number one, go to the states. Every state can entertain lawsuits, even against the federal government. You can have the attorney general or local prosecutor bring a lawsuit.
paragraphs.cn: 仅基于司法部目前的立场，他们有意保护国土安全部，所以可能性很低，但他们可以这样做。这是他们的权利。但还有另外两条途径。很快说一下，奥迪，如果他们认真对待的话，还有两条前进的途径。第一，去各州。每个州都可以受理诉讼，甚至针对联邦政府。你可以让州检察长或地方检察官提起诉讼。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: But also Congress can oversee and step in if ICE is behaving in a manner that's not appropriate.
paragraphs.cn: 但如果ICE的行为不当，国会也可以进行监督并介入。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, right now, ICE related Mexican national deaths in Trump's term, 14 people in ICE custody, three in ICE operations. Sheinbaum had more or less kind of played nice with Trump when it came to tariffs, right, and when it came to the drug war. Why do you think, Lulu, that she is speaking out now and in such a specific way, using the phrase targeted?
paragraphs.cn: 那么，目前特朗普任期内与ICE相关的墨西哥国民死亡人数为：14人在ICE拘留期间死亡，3人在ICE行动中死亡。辛鲍姆在关税和毒品战争问题上或多或少对特朗普表现出了友好态度。卢卢，你认为她为什么现在以如此具体的方式发声，并使用了"针对"这个词？

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Yes. This is always a hugely sensitive issue for Mexico. Let's not forget that, you know, Mexico has the largest number of immigrants in the United States, and they share a border. And so, the way that ICE and Border Patrol interact with that population has always been a source of enormous sensitivity and tension.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。这对墨西哥来说一直是一个非常敏感的问题。别忘了，墨西哥在美国的移民数量最多，而且两国共享边界。因此，ICE和边境巡逻队与该群体的互动方式一直是高度敏感和紧张的根源。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And I think one of the reasons they're doing this is not only because they think they're going to get satisfaction, but it is to be perceived by their own people as doing something, as raising complaints, as trying to actively engage.
paragraphs.cn: 我认为他们这样做的一个原因不仅是他们认为会得到满意的结果，而且是为了让本国人民看到他们在采取行动、提出投诉、并积极介入。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Because it's not only that the government is in this very difficult position with trying to appease Trump and work with him while also defending its population, but the people themselves do not like the Trump administration, do not like the way that this is playing out, and want their government to do something.
paragraphs.cn: 因为不仅政府处于非常困难的境地，既要安抚特朗普并与之合作，又要保护本国人民，而且人民本身也不喜欢特朗普政府，不喜欢事态的发展，希望他们的政府有所作为。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And so, this is a way of them showing that they are.
paragraphs.cn: 因此，这是他们展示自己正在行动的一种方式。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And it raises the profile of ICE operations in a moment when they had effectively lowered the temperature after the mass protests in two separate cities, and deaths on the streets.
paragraphs.cn: 而在两个不同城市的大规模抗议和街头死亡事件之后，ICE实际上已经降低了紧张局势，此举又提高了ICE行动的关注度。

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.en: Yes. Well, look, I -- my message to the president of Mexico, Claudia Sheinbaum, is, game on. You know, you want to sue over this. I guess we'll have to sue over the hundreds of thousands of young men and women in this country that have died from fentanyl overdoses or even the hundreds of people that have had violence inflicted on them that have been raped and sexually assaulted. You know, Claudia Sheinbaum is one to talk. She was elected in a year where there were over 27 political assassinations in her party.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。好吧，我想对墨西哥总统克劳迪娅·辛鲍姆说：放马过来。你想就此提起诉讼。我想我们也不得不对这个国家因芬太尼过量死亡的数十万年轻人，甚至遭受暴力、强奸和性侵犯的数百人提起诉讼。克劳迪娅·辛鲍姆说得倒轻巧。她当选的那一年，她的政党内有超过27起政治暗杀事件。

paragraphs.speaker: Terry Schilling
paragraphs.en: She's literally a hand-picked candidate for the cartels. And again --
paragraphs.cn: 她简直就是贩毒集团精心挑选的候选人。而且 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You've levied a lot of different things there.
paragraphs.cn: 你刚才提出了很多不同的指控。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: So, I want to hold on because I'm not fact checking you on that level, OK. But I do have this question about, is it fair for a country to say, you're killing our nationals on your street and that this is state sanctioned deaths, and therefore.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，我想先说明，我不会在这个层面上核实你的说法，好吧。但我确实有个问题：一个国家说'你们在我们的街道上杀害我们的国民，这是国家批准的死亡'，这样说公平吗？因此……

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Well, look, I noticed in that chart, in 2023 and 2024 there were -- I think in 2023 it said there were seven deaths by ICE and 11 deaths in 2024. That's when there was like no immigration enforcement at all. And it's only around 50 between these last two years. There it goes, 33 and 19 in two years. That's 52.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，你看，我注意到那张图表上，2023年和2024年 -- -- 我记得2023年显示有7人死于ICE，2024年有11人。那是在几乎没有移民执法的情况下。而过去两年只有大约50人。你看，两年内33人和19人，总共52人。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: That's when we're actually deporting people and actually enforcing the law. So, it seems like there's some violence embedded into illegal immigration in the first place.
paragraphs.cn: 那是在我们实际驱逐人员、执行法律的时候。所以，似乎非法移民本身就带有一些暴力。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: I -- well, no, no, no. I -- the only thing I will say to sort of push back on that gently is, and I worked at -- I was a senior official at ICE for five years. You're still talking about deporting 400,000 people a year, even under the low points of any administration's immigration enforcement efforts. So, the idea that enforcement simply isn't happening, and those are, I believe we were talking about detention centers, people are detained, beds are full.
paragraphs.cn: 我 -- -- 嗯，不，不，不。我唯一想温和地反驳的是，我在ICE担任高级官员五年。即使在任何政府移民执法工作的低谷期，你仍然在谈论每年驱逐40万人。所以，认为执法根本没有发生的想法，以及那些 -- -- 我相信我们讨论的是拘留中心，人们被拘留，床位是满的。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: It's just not accurate that no -- that no enforcement was happening. Now, certainly we can agree, and I think we should, that more enforcement is happening now than happened in 2023. But I -- but I don't think that that -- those numbers are reflective of enforcement simply not happening in detention centers.
paragraphs.cn: 说没有执法发生是不准确的。当然，我们可以同意，而且我认为应该同意，现在的执法比2023年更多。但我不认为那些数字反映了拘留中心根本没有执法的情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: So, a lot of people are detained by the United States.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，很多人被美国拘留。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And I'll also say that they -- (CROSS TALK)
paragraphs.cn: 我还要说，他们 -- -- （交叉对话）

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And I will say that the implication --
paragraphs.cn: 我要说的是，这种暗示 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: And a lot more people were coming into the United States in those years.
paragraphs.cn: 而且那些年有更多的人进入美国。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Yes, but I'll also say that the implication that somehow enforcement is violent and therefore these deaths are somehow acceptable is --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，但我也要说，那种暗示执法是暴力的，因此这些死亡在某种程度上是可以接受的，这是 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And not accept -- well, let's pause on this. It's not acceptable, certainly, to the families. And as we started this story with somebody who's lost their father when they weren't even the target of the operation. So, I think the question of, what standard do we hold ourselves to is coming up in this. I want you guys to stay with me. We're going to turn to another story in the future talking about NASA, because NASA is now recruiting volunteers for a yearlong moon and mars simulation.
paragraphs.cn: 而且不接受 -- -- 好吧，我们在这里暂停一下。这对家属来说当然是不能接受的。正如我们开始这个故事时提到的那样，有人失去了父亲，而他们甚至不是行动的目标。所以，我认为我们对自己持有什么标准的问题正在浮现。请各位继续关注。我们接下来要转向另一个关于NASA的故事，因为NASA正在招募志愿者进行为期一年的月球和火星模拟任务。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Voters will be in space-like -- sorry, volunteers will be in space-like conditions, living inside confined habitats and carrying out tasks, just as astronauts would at the start of the year.
paragraphs.cn: 选民们将处于类似太空的环境 -- -- 抱歉，志愿者们将处于类似太空的环境中，生活在封闭的栖息地内，执行任务，就像宇航员在年初所做的那样。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: An alleged plot by Iran to assassinate President Trump uncovered by Israel. Sources tell CNN they shared the intelligence with the U.S. this week. I'm bringing in "New York Times" White House correspondent Maggie Haberman. She's co-author of the new bestseller "Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump." Maggie, thank you so much for being here.
paragraphs.cn: 以色列发现了一起伊朗涉嫌暗杀特朗普总统的阴谋。消息人士告诉CNN，他们本周与美国分享了这一情报。我邀请《纽约时报》白宫记者玛吉·哈伯曼。她是畅销新书《政权更迭：特朗普帝国总统内幕》的合著者。玛吉，非常感谢你的到来。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: This seems like a good week to have this conversation between the inflection point ending the ceasefire and then this moment of this reporting we're getting now of a potential assassination attempt, information shared by Israel. This is a president who has survived assassination attempts.
paragraphs.cn: 这周似乎是进行这场对话的好时机，从停火协议的转折点结束，到我们现在得到的关于潜在暗杀企图的报道，这些信息由以色列分享。这是一位曾多次躲过暗杀企图的总统。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Does this kind of concern loom large for him? MAGGIE HABERMAN, WHITE HOUSE CORRESPONDENT, "THE NEW YORK TIMES": Well, there's no question that among the reasons that the president and his team were really radicalized in the interregnum period before he came back to office, and we write about this in the book, were the assassination attempts that he faced.
paragraphs.cn: 这种担忧对他而言是否显得格外突出？《纽约时报》白宫记者玛吉·哈伯曼：毫无疑问，总统及其团队在他重返白宫前的过渡期变得激进的原因之一，就是他所面临的暗杀企图，我们在书中也写到了这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: In particular, he -- Butler happened around the time that someone had been charged with being part of a murder for hire plot involving -- or set up by Iranian officials allegedly. And in his mind, and in the campaign's mind at the time, it all became one thing. And so, there's no question that the fact that this new information about a potential plot is going to loom large for this team. It is going to raise new concerns.
paragraphs.cn: 特别是，巴特勒事件发生前后，有人被指控参与了一起涉及伊朗官员策划的买凶杀人阴谋。在他和当时的竞选团队看来，这一切都成了一回事。因此，毫无疑问，关于潜在阴谋的新信息将对这个团队产生重大影响。这将引发新的担忧。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You are already seeing a president who, Audie, does not travel outside the White House that much. Part of it is that, you know, he would tell aides at the beginning of the -- of the term, I'm done campaigning. But part of it are -- is legitimate security concerns.
paragraphs.cn: 你已经看到一位总统，奥迪，他不太离开白宫。部分原因是，你知道，他在任期开始时告诉助手，我已经完成了竞选。但部分原因是出于合理的安全考虑。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's interesting talk -- hearing from you in this moment because he has such a different cast of characters around him compared to term one. How does that affect something like prosecuting this war, where we see like while people are having talks, he says, look, ceasefire is over, and all of a sudden we are in the middle of strikes again.
paragraphs.cn: 有趣的是，此刻听你说话，因为他身边的人物与第一任期相比截然不同。这如何影响像推进这场战争这样的事情？我们看到，当人们进行谈判时，他说，看，停火结束了，然后突然间我们又陷入了空袭之中。

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.en: So, there's a couple of things, Audie. One is that this government, unlike term one, and one of the things that we write about very clearly is how radically different this term is than the first term. The decisions are being made by a small handful of about a half a dozen advisors. And if someone is not in the room with those advisors in the government, they often have no idea what is going on.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，有几点，奥迪。一是这届政府与第一任期不同，我们在书中非常清楚地写到这一任期与第一任期有多么截然不同。决策由大约六名顾问中的一小部分人做出。如果某人不在政府中与这些顾问同处一室，他们往往对正在发生的事情一无所知。

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.en: And that is generally by design, including Steve Witkoff, who is Trump's envoy for everything, who had been negotiating this ceasefire or some kind of an Iranian deal for some time. But we also describe how last year Steve Witkoff thought that he was getting close to a deal, and then Trump made some other pronouncement, and it became very clear that foreign policy, as someone said, you know, and we quote them anonymously in the book, is, whatever Trump says at any given moment.
paragraphs.cn: 这通常是刻意为之，包括史蒂夫·维特科夫，他是特朗普的全面特使，一段时间以来一直在谈判停火协议或某种伊朗协议。但我们也描述了去年史蒂夫·维特科夫如何认为自己接近达成协议，然后特朗普发表了其他声明，结果很明显，外交政策，正如某人所说，我们在书中匿名引用了他的话，就是特朗普在任何时刻所说的任何话。

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.en: And so, you are seeing that right now with this ceasefire where I think it is -- it is most problematic is for diplomat -- long-term diplomatic conversations, incremental comments matter, mixed signals matter. And that has obviously happened for some time here. This is also a war that the president clearly wants to be done with. And there just is no quick fix. And that is frustrating him.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，你现在看到的正是这种情况，在这项停火协议中，我认为最成问题的是外交 -- -- 长期的外交对话中，渐进式的言论很重要，混杂的信号也很重要。而这种情况显然已经持续了一段时间。这也是一场总统显然希望结束的战争，但就是没有快速解决办法。这让他感到沮丧。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I want to turn to another issue because immigration has been obviously a defining issue for Trump in both terms, but arguably he's probably made the most gains in this term. Largely, Stephen Miller has looked at as the architect of that. Can you talk to me about the, where we are now, meaning Noem is gone, we were just talking about Mexico complaining about Mexican nationals being targeted. Is this something that the architects around Trump -- that it's by design?
paragraphs.cn: 我想转向另一个问题，因为移民问题显然是特朗普两届任期内的标志性议题，但可以说他在本届任期内取得的进展最大。很大程度上，斯蒂芬·米勒被视为这一政策的策划者。你能跟我谈谈我们现在的情况吗？也就是说，诺姆已经离职，我们刚才还在讨论墨西哥抱怨墨西哥国民成为目标。这是特朗普身边的策划者们有意为之吗？

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.en: So, we have seen a lot of fits and starts with immigration efforts and policy because there was such a flood the zone effort. Stephen Miller spent several years preparing for a second Trump term. He looked at all of the levers of government. He had it -- he has an actual plan for what he wanted to do. He is more hard line on immigration than Trump is, although Trump obviously only tends to react and, you know, reel Miller in when there's negative media coverage.
paragraphs.cn: 我们看到移民政策和行动经历了很多波折，因为当时有大量涌入的尝试。斯蒂芬·米勒花了数年时间为特朗普的第二任期做准备。他研究了政府的各个杠杆。他有一个实际计划，知道自己想做什么。他在移民问题上比特朗普更强硬，尽管特朗普显然只在负面媒体报道出现时才会做出反应，并约束米勒。

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.en: And so, you saw that earlier this year with the shooting deaths of Renee Good and Alex Pretti. And these showy ICE sweeps into cities, these, you know, dramatic displays that the administration would clip and put out in social media videos, those slowed down, but that doesn't mean that the efforts slowed down. And so, you are going -- you are seeing -- my colleagues have written about this at "The Times," larger sweeps. You are seeing -- they're just broadcast with less vigor and intensity.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，你在今年早些时候看到了蕾妮·古德和亚历克斯·普雷蒂被枪杀的事件。还有那些引人注目的ICE突袭城市行动，那些戏剧性的展示，政府会剪辑并发布到社交媒体视频中，这些行动放缓了，但这并不意味着努力放缓了。所以，你会看到 -- -- 我的同事在《纽约时报》上写过 -- -- 更大规模的突袭。你会看到 -- -- 只是宣传的力度和强度降低了。

paragraphs.speaker: Haberman
paragraphs.en: It calmed the protests down at the time. I don't know where this goes now. But they are trying now -- Stephen Miller had a target of 3,000 arrests a day. That was not doable. Now there is a smaller target, but it's still quite high. And he is very intent on pushing this ahead for as long as he can.
paragraphs.cn: 这当时平息了抗议活动。我不知道现在会走向何方。但他们现在正在尝试 -- -- 斯蒂芬·米勒的目标是每天逮捕3000人。这不可行。现在目标更小了，但仍然相当高。他非常执着地要尽可能长时间地推进这件事。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, that's Maggie Haberman. Her new book with Jonathan Swan is called "Regime Change: Inside the Imperial Presidency of Donald Trump." It's out now. I also want to turn to this, the ballot for governor in Colorado, it's set. Marine veteran Victor Marx secured the top Republican spot when the race was called last night. Both of his GOP rivals in the race have said they won't support him in the general election, calling him, quote, "a disaster" and a "con man." VICTOR MARX (R),
paragraphs.cn: 好的，那是玛吉·哈伯曼。她与乔纳森·斯旺合著的新书名为《政权更迭：唐纳德·特朗普的帝王式总统任期内部》，现已出版。我还想谈谈科罗拉多州州长初选，结果已定。海军陆战队老兵维克多·马克思在昨晚的竞选中获得了共和党头号候选人的位置。他的两位共和党对手都表示不会在大选中支持他，称他为"灾难"和"骗子"。维克多·马克思（共和党人）说：

paragraphs.speaker: Colorado Candidate For Governor
paragraphs.en: So, if you are frustrated, skeptical or wondering if anything can actually change, I just proved it by winning the primary.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，如果你感到沮丧、怀疑或想知道是否真的能有所改变，我刚刚通过赢得初选证明了这一点。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: OK, group chat is back. This is a coda. I'm sorry, Terry, this is a coda.
paragraphs.cn: 好的，群聊又回来了。这是一个尾声。抱歉，特里，这是一个尾声。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You and I talked about this in -- last week. And you were like, look, ignore people like this. This was in our Platner discussion.
paragraphs.cn: 你和我上周讨论过这个。你说，看，别理会这种人。这是在我们普拉特纳讨论中提到的。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And you were like, look, this is not a guy that you should be focusing on. He is now the GOP candidate for governor in Colorado.
paragraphs.cn: 你说，看，这不是你应该关注的人。他现在是科罗拉多州共和党州长候选人。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: In Colorado, in Illinois, in New York, in California.
paragraphs.cn: 在科罗拉多州、伊利诺伊州、纽约州、加利福尼亚州。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: But also two Republicans in the race.
paragraphs.cn: 但还有两名共和党人也参加了竞选。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Sour grapes or them literally being like, this disaster is a con man.
paragraphs.cn: 是酸葡萄心理，还是他们真的觉得这场灾难是个骗子？

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Again -- look, again, I think one of the biggest travesties is when these blue states become so blue that they're not competitive. And when they're not competitive, you don't get the quality candidates that you need actually to win.
paragraphs.cn: 再说一遍 -- -- 你看，我认为最大的悲剧之一就是这些蓝州变得如此蓝，以至于没有竞争性。当它们没有竞争性时，你就得不到真正需要获胜的优质候选人。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: No, no, no, Terry, he raised $3 million. That's not ate (ph) money from Democrats.
paragraphs.cn: 不，不，不，特里，他筹集了300万美元。那不是来自民主党人的钱。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: I know. That's not very much for -- but for a governor race --
paragraphs.cn: 我知道。对于州长竞选来说，这不算多 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Schilling
paragraphs.en: You know, you can raise that just by going on a few punchy podcasts. It's really not that much money for even a congressional race at this point.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，你只要上几个火爆的播客就能筹到那么多钱。现在这点钱甚至对于国会竞选来说都不算多。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Right. He claims to have rescued 45,000 abuse victims. He performs prayers to free people of demonic oppression. Claims to have called an airstrike on ISIS. Says he was forced to kill a man at age seven. Who among us?
paragraphs.cn: 没错。他声称拯救了4.5万名虐待受害者。他进行祈祷以解放受恶魔压迫的人。声称曾对ISIS发动空袭。说自己七岁时被迫杀了一个人。我们中有谁这样？

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Well, look, we got Abigail Spanberger, who's an admitted CIA operative, as the governor of Virginia. I'd like to know what she's done in her career.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，你看，我们有阿比盖尔·斯潘伯格，她是一名公开的中情局特工，担任弗吉尼亚州州长。我想知道她在职业生涯中做了什么。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: From the -- the CIA does some pretty gnarly stuff.
paragraphs.cn: 中情局干过一些相当恶劣的事情。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: And you don't want to know about demonic --
paragraphs.cn: 你不会想听关于恶魔的 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Ok. Ok. Schilling
paragraphs.en: No, I definitely want to know about that. I believe in the spiritual world.
paragraphs.cn: 不，我绝对想听。我相信灵界的存在。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I mean, work with me, Terry. Work with me. Lulu, the reason why I'm talking about this is because candidate quality, a term that we heard from Mitch McConnell.
paragraphs.cn: 我是说，配合我一下，特里。配合我。露露，我之所以谈这个，是因为候选人素质 -- -- 我们从米奇·麦康奈尔那里听到的一个词。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: You know what I mean? It's been so on the minds of everyone with the Graham Platner thing in the Senate. And just like in Republican circles, in Colorado, the voters want what the voters want. What have we learned about the limits of that?
paragraphs.cn: 你懂我的意思吗？参议院里格雷厄姆·普拉特纳的事情让每个人都忧心忡忡。就像在共和党圈子里，在科罗拉多州，选民想要什么就是什么。关于这一点，我们学到了什么教训？

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: We've learned that demonic possession is a thing, clearly.
paragraphs.cn: 我们学到了恶魔附身是真实存在的，显然。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: That is -- that is -- that is --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Also teleporting to a Waffle House. A different story.
paragraphs.cn: 还有瞬移到华夫饼屋。那是另一个故事。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And teleporting to a Waffle House. I mean -- listen, this is a huge issue, I think, for both Republicans and Democrats and what I think any -- or what I hear from both my Democratic and Republican friends who are in the political system in that way is that they say, well, it's because the parties are so weak and we no longer get to decide about quality candidates -- you know, candidate quality. These decisions used to be made in backrooms. And so we could really vet people and put the best candidate forward.
paragraphs.cn: 还有瞬移到华夫饼屋。我是说 -- -- 听着，我认为这对共和党和民主党来说都是个大问题，我从那些在政治体系中的民主党和共和党朋友那里听到的是，他们说，嗯，这是因为政党太弱了，我们不再能决定候选人素质 -- -- 你知道，候选人素质。这些决定过去是在幕后做出的。所以我们可以真正审查候选人，推出最佳人选。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And that is true. What voters will say is, yay, I'm so glad that this is no longer being done in backrooms.
paragraphs.cn: 确实如此。选民会说，耶，我很高兴这不再在幕后进行了。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: I know, but how come some people can ride it out and some people can't? Now, Platner is accused of violence. I think that clearly turned out to be a red line. But there's lots of other people dealing with scandals. Ken Paxton in Texas.
paragraphs.cn: 我知道，但为什么有些人能挺过去，有些人不能？现在，普拉特纳被指控暴力。我认为那显然是一条红线。但还有很多其他人面临丑闻。比如得克萨斯州的肯·帕克斯顿。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Obviously, Marx. I mean his -- one of the people he ran against said she ran a good race and, quote, "for the record, I still haven't killed anyone." It seems as though --
paragraphs.cn: 显然，马克思。我是说，他的 -- -- 他的一位竞选对手说她打了一场漂亮的仗，并且引用原话："郑重声明，我还没杀过人。"看起来 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, that's the bar.
paragraphs.cn: 是的，这就是标准。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: What is the bar for voters?
paragraphs.cn: 选民的标准是什么？

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: You know, I mean, we have to understand that primary voters in any party are the most activist and sort of most partisan of any voters in the country.
paragraphs.cn: 你知道，我的意思是，我们必须明白，任何政党的初选选民都是全国最活跃、最党派化的选民。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: That's why we should have open primaries.
paragraphs.cn: 这就是为什么我们应该实行开放式初选。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: That's why we should have open primaries or ranked choice voting, you know, as you have -- as you have in --
paragraphs.cn: 这就是为什么我们应该实行开放式初选或排序投票，就像你们 -- -- 就像你们在 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Or ranked choice voting.
paragraphs.cn: 或者排序投票。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Oh, they hate -- the parties hate ranked choice voting.
paragraphs.cn: 哦，他们讨厌 -- -- 政党讨厌排序投票。

paragraphs.speaker: Williams
paragraphs.en: Well, but my point -- sure, but all I'm saying is that when the most activist people are the ones casting the votes, you're going to occasionally get stuff like this in --
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，但我的观点是 -- -- 当然，我只是想说，当最活跃的人投票时，你偶尔会遇到这种情况 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: It's not only that --
paragraphs.cn: 不仅如此 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: But to your point, actually, when you have one party rule in a state, the opposition party becomes very radicalized I think. We see that in California with the GOP. We see that, you know, in Democratic politics and sort of right leaning states. And so, I do think that you get like the most hardcore people putting candidates forward.
paragraphs.cn: 但说到你的观点，实际上，当一个州由一党统治时，反对党会变得非常激进，我认为是这样。我们在加州看到共和党就是这样。我们看到，你知道，在民主党政治和偏右的州也是如此。所以，我确实认为你会看到最核心的人推举候选人。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: And we are in an era, we are led by a populist, we are led by someone who was a celebrity, who has innumerable sort of scandals, both previous and current, and I think it's opened the door --
paragraphs.cn: 我们处在一个时代，由一位民粹主义者领导，由一位曾是名人、有无数的丑闻（无论是过去还是现在）的人领导，我认为这打开了大门 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: Yes, but what we have -- we've learned, while he may be --
paragraphs.cn: 是的，但我们所知道的是，虽然他可能 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: While he may be scandal proof, not everyone else is. That's what we have learned.
paragraphs.cn: 虽然他可能对丑闻免疫，但其他人不是。这就是我们学到的。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: Yes. But maybe. But maybe not.
paragraphs.cn: 是的。但也许吧。也许不是。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I mean, I think everyone's trying their game and they're trying to see how far they can push it until voters or the parties or people just say, the establishment says enough is enough. Rape seems to -- accusations of rape seem to be that line for the Democrats, but that certainly hasn't been the line for Republicans.
paragraphs.cn: 我的意思是，我认为每个人都在尝试自己的策略，他们试图看看自己能走多远，直到选民或政党或人们，或者说建制派说够了。强奸似乎 -- -- 强奸指控似乎是民主党的底线，但这肯定不是共和党的底线。

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: Well, it was the line until it became clear that Platner was actually weakened and was most likely going to lose. The Democrats did not care about Lyndsey Fifield when she made her accusations because she was a Republican.
paragraphs.cn: 嗯，这曾经是底线，直到普拉特纳明显被削弱并且很可能失败。民主党不在乎林赛·菲尔德提出指控，因为她是共和党人。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I'm -- what I'm trying to -- what I'm --

paragraphs.speaker: Schilling
paragraphs.en: So, it's, believe all Democrat women, not believe any Republican women.
paragraphs.cn: 所以，就是相信所有民主党女性，不相信任何共和党女性。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: What I'm -- what I'm trying to say is, you have a president here who has many, many allegations. All I'm saying --
paragraphs.cn: 我想说的是，你有一位总统，他有很多很多指控。我只是说 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: It's funny, we are ending the week the way we started it, almost with the exact same discussion is that --
paragraphs.cn: 有趣的是，我们以本周开始的方式结束了这一周，几乎完全相同的讨论 -- --

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I'm not arguing with you.
paragraphs.cn: 我不是在跟你争论。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: No, no, it's the same beats I hear partisan wise.
paragraphs.cn: 不，不，我听到的党派论调还是老一套。

paragraphs.speaker: Garcia-Navarro
paragraphs.en: I'm just saying is, what's good for the goose is good for the gander. And I think both Republicans and Democrats need to -- need to actually grapple with this. This isn't like a he said/she said.
paragraphs.cn: 我只是想说，一视同仁。我认为共和党和民主党都需要 -- -- 实际上需要认真对待这个问题。这不像那种各执一词的情况。

paragraphs.speaker: Cornish
paragraphs.en: We hope to have more news for you next week. Thank you for being in the group chat. Thank you for waking up with us. A lot of places you can spend your time, and you choose to spend it with us. I really appreciate it. I'm Audie Cornish, and the headlines are next. var CNN = CNN || {};
paragraphs.cn: 我们希望下周能为您带来更多新闻。感谢您参与群聊。感谢您和我们一起醒来。您有很多地方可以花时间，却选择和我们在一起。我真的很感激。我是奥蒂·科尼什，接下来是新闻头条。

vocabulary.en: a light frame for carrying sick or injured people
vocabulary.excerpt: Neighbors saw the senator being loaded into an ambulance on a stretcher.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 邻居们看到参议员被用担架抬上救护车。

vocabulary.en: the quality of being open and honest; not hiding anything
vocabulary.excerpt: It's bringing up questions about transparency surrounding his health.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这引发了关于其健康状况透明度的质疑。

vocabulary.en: information about the plans of an enemy or opponent
vocabulary.excerpt: Israel shares intel of an Iran plant -- plan to assassinate President Trump.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 以色列分享了伊朗计划暗杀特朗普总统的情报。

vocabulary.en: to murder a prominent person, often for political reasons
vocabulary.excerpt: Israel shares intel of an Iran plant -- plan to assassinate President Trump.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 以色列分享了伊朗计划暗杀特朗普总统的情报。

vocabulary.en: a sudden or unexpected change or exchange
vocabulary.excerpt: Does that explain the plane switcheroo and the renewed strikes by the U.S.?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这能解释飞机调换和美国恢复打击吗？

vocabulary.en: bending or collapsing under pressure
vocabulary.excerpt: Urgent work continues to save that buckling building in New York from partial collapse.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 紧急工作持续进行，以拯救纽约那栋弯曲变形的建筑免于部分坍塌。

vocabulary.en: a collapse that affects only part of a structure
vocabulary.excerpt: Urgent work continues to save that buckling building in New York from partial collapse.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 紧急工作持续进行，以拯救纽约那栋弯曲变形的建筑免于部分坍塌。

vocabulary.en: a strong negative reaction to a recent event or development
vocabulary.excerpt: Kylie's Meta Collaboration Sparks Backlash.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 凯莉与Meta的合作引发强烈反对。

vocabulary.cn: 制造共识（指通过媒体等操控公众同意）
vocabulary.en: a concept from media theory referring to the process by which media shape public opinion to accept certain policies or ideas
vocabulary.excerpt: They are manufacturing consent.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他们在制造共识。

vocabulary.cn: 残忍的；病态的；对死亡感兴趣的
vocabulary.en: showing a morbid interest in death or disaster
vocabulary.excerpt: One of the reasons why people are talking about this, political people are talking about this in a kind of ghoulish way, frankly.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 人们谈论此事的原因之一，坦率说，政治人物以一种病态的方式在谈论。

vocabulary.cn: 席位空缺法（规定如何填补议员空缺的法律）
vocabulary.en: a law that specifies the procedure for filling a vacant legislative seat
vocabulary.excerpt: the Kentucky Senate vacancy law, meaning what do you have to do if suddenly a Senate seat is open?
vocabulary.exampleCn: 肯塔基州参议院席位空缺法，意思是如果参议院席位突然空缺，你必须做什么？

vocabulary.en: an election held to fill a vacancy that occurs between regular elections
vocabulary.excerpt: Well, there's a special election.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 嗯，会有一场特别选举。

vocabulary.en: officially recognized as meeting certain standards
vocabulary.excerpt: And the seat has to be empty until a winner is certified.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 在获胜者被认证之前，席位必须保持空缺。

vocabulary.en: a pretense or false show
vocabulary.excerpt: This charade can't continue.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 这种装模作样不能再继续了。

vocabulary.en: advanced cancer of the intestine that has spread to other parts of the body
vocabulary.excerpt: he was diagnosed with stage four intestinal cancer.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他被诊断出四期肠癌。

vocabulary.cn: 连贯的；清晰的；有逻辑的
vocabulary.en: logical and consistent; able to speak clearly
vocabulary.excerpt: Scott Jennings has backed up that he's completely fine, and he's coherent.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 斯科特·詹宁斯证实他完全没事，而且思路清晰。

vocabulary.en: a lot of fuss over something trivial
vocabulary.excerpt: But I think this is much ado about nothing.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 但我认为这是小题大做。

vocabulary.cn: 消除；避免；使成为不必要
vocabulary.en: to remove a need or difficulty
vocabulary.excerpt: the fact that there isn't an election somehow obviates the senator from one transparency or to doing his job.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 没有选举这一事实并不能免除参议员的透明度义务或履职责任。

vocabulary.en: to control the schedule for political advantage
vocabulary.excerpt: he was someone who manipulated the calendar a lot
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他是一个经常操纵日程的人。

vocabulary.en: to join a discussion or argument
vocabulary.excerpt: Meghan McCain weighed in, as well.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 梅根·麦凯恩也发表了意见。

vocabulary.en: legal restrictions on the number of terms an elected official can serve
vocabulary.excerpt: I'm now an advocate of age and term limits in politics.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我现在是政治中年龄和任期限制的倡导者。

vocabulary.en: beliefs that events are secretly manipulated by powerful groups
vocabulary.excerpt: leading to these conspiracies.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 导致了这些阴谋论。

vocabulary.en: kind and forgiving in judging others
vocabulary.excerpt: I think there's a charitable way to read this
vocabulary.exampleCn: 我认为有一种善意的解读方式。

vocabulary.en: a period of time during which something happens or is expected to happen
vocabulary.excerpt: He works on his own time frame.
vocabulary.exampleCn: 他按照自己的时间表行事。

sentences.en: We've got new video just into CNN, showing the moment Mitch McConnell was taken to the hospital a month ago.
sentences.cn: 我们刚刚收到一段新视频，显示一个月前米奇·麦康奈尔被送往医院的那一刻。
sentences.structure: 主句（We've got new video）+ 后置定语（just into CNN）+ 现在分词短语（showing...）作定语修饰video，其中包含省略that的定语从句（Mitch McConnell was taken...）修饰moment。
sentences.analysis: showing the moment...是现在分词短语作后置定语，相当于which shows...；moment后接定语从句，省略了关系副词when。

sentences.en: Neighbors saw the senator being loaded into an ambulance on a stretcher.
sentences.cn: 邻居们看到这位参议员被用担架抬上救护车。
sentences.structure: 主谓宾（Neighbors saw）+ 宾语补足语（the senator being loaded...），其中being loaded是现在分词被动形式。
sentences.analysis: see sb doing sth表示看到某人正在做某事，这里用被动形式being loaded强调动作的承受者。

sentences.en: One of the reasons why people are talking about this, political people are talking about this in a kind of ghoulish way, frankly, is that the Kentucky Senate vacancy law, meaning what do you have to do if suddenly a Senate seat is open?
sentences.cn: 人们谈论此事的原因之一——政治人物以一种近乎病态的方式谈论此事，坦率地说——是肯塔基州参议院空缺法，即如果参议院席位突然空缺，你必须做什么？
sentences.structure: 主句（One of the reasons...is that...），其中reasons后接why引导的定语从句，插入语（political people...frankly）分隔主谓，that引导表语从句，表语从句中meaning...是现在分词短语作解释。
sentences.analysis: why引导定语从句修饰reasons；插入语增加口语化色彩；meaning...相当于which means...，对law进行解释。

sentences.en: The idea is that the clock is somehow ticking on if, suddenly, there's an open seat in Kentucky, whether it can be filled.
sentences.cn: 这个想法是，如果肯塔基州突然出现一个空缺席位，时钟就在滴答作响，无论它能否被填补。
sentences.structure: 主句（The idea is that...），that引导表语从句，表语从句中包含if引导的条件状语从句和whether引导的让步状语从句。
sentences.analysis: the clock is ticking是比喻，表示时间紧迫；whether引导让步状语从句，意为“无论是否”。

sentences.en: What I don't know is why Republicans are kind of leading the charge online, talking about this.
sentences.cn: 我不知道的是，为什么共和党人似乎在网上带头讨论这件事。
sentences.structure: 主语从句（What I don't know）+ 系动词is + 表语从句（why Republicans...），现在分词短语（talking about this）作伴随状语。
sentences.analysis: What引导主语从句；why引导表语从句；leading the charge是习语，意为“带头冲锋”；kind of表示“有点，似乎”。

sentences.en: If McConnell is in as bad a shape as Biden ever was, or worse, he needs to step aside.
sentences.cn: 如果麦康奈尔的身体状况和拜登一样糟糕，甚至更糟，他就需要让位。
